title: Our-side Zephyr HAL foundation for firmware targets
description:
## Why
Before a dev partner can efficiently add many board ports, LICHEN needs a stable Zephyr HAL contract and a small set of reference targets that define how firmware code discovers and uses board capabilities. Without this, partner work will hard-code board quirks into application logic and make UI, radio, location/time, power, and app-interface behavior inconsistent across targets.

## Scope
Define the HAL architecture, board capability model, Kconfig/devicetree conventions, reference target implementations, validation matrix, and partner handoff package. Scope is our side of the work: own the abstraction boundary and reference implementations; leave long-tail board overlays and hardware smoke testing to partner Beads once the contract is stable.

## Success Criteria
Child Beads cover HAL contract, capability matrix, Kconfig/devicetree model, reference targets, source-provider integration, validation, and partner handoff. Meshtastic and MeshCore board/HAL patterns are used as source material for capability modeling and board prioritization, while LICHEN remains Zephyr-native. Two code-review passes are completed before closing any implementation child.
